content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN elements and an integer KKK. You have to find the subarray with the maximum sum among all the K-sized sub-arrays and output this maximum sum.

This is a perfect example of a question that can be efficiently solved using the sliding window approach.

Normally, we would calculate the sum of all K-sized sub-arrays which would have the time complexity of O(N)O(N)O(N).

But, using the Sliding Window approach we can assume the K-sized subarray to be a window. Now we just need to calculate the sum of the first window as the sum of the next window can be derived from the previous sum by subtracting the first element and adding the next element.

For example, let's take an array A=[1,2,3,4,5,6,7]A = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]A=[1,2,3,4,5,6,7] and K=3K = 3K=3.


Let's calculate the sum of the first window of three elements. 1+2+3=61+2+3 = 61+2+3=6
Now the next window will have element 2,3,4{2, 3, 4}2,3,4.
Compared to the previous window the first element (1)(1)(1) is removed and the next element (4)(4)(4) is added.
So, we can calculate the next window's sum by subtracting 111 and adding 444 to the sum of the previous window.
Thus, the sum of the next window will be 6−1+4=96-1+4 = 96−1+4=9
Solve the above question using the Sliding Window approach


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN and KKK, denoting the length of the array AAA and the length of the sub-array.
The second line of the input contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — denoting the array AAA.


Output Format

Output the maximum sum.
constraints: All sums fit in 64‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line of a test case contains two integers N and K — the length of the array and the size of the sub‑array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1,A2,…,AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible sum of any contiguous sub‑array of length K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The window {9,9,5} (positions 6‑8) gives the maximum sum 23.
The last window {4,5} has sum 9, which is the largest.

solution.approach: The sum of a K‑sized window can be updated in O(1) when we move the window one position to the right: subtract the element that leaves the window and add the new element that enters. Compute the sum of the first window, store it as the current best, then slide the window across the array updating the sum and the best whenever a larger sum is found. This runs in linear time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the input array

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        n=int(next(it)); k=int(next(it))
        arr=[int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        cur=sum(arr[:k])
        best=cur
        for i in range(k,n):
            cur+=arr[i]-arr[i-k]
            if cur>best:
                best=cur
        out.append(str(best))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
